output.dependency: The structured flight identifier appears to encode a specific flight route/service, and it is associated with the same scheduled departure timestamp. In the sample, the same airline-flight-route code corresponds to the same departure date/time string.

The structured flight identifier appears to determine the actual or updated departure timestamp. The same airline-flight-route code is paired with the same nearby departure date/time value in both rows.
The structured flight identifier appears to determine the scheduled arrival time. In the sample, the same airline-flight-route code corresponds to the same arrival time-of-day value.
The structured flight identifier appears to determine the actual or updated arrival time. The same airline-flight-route code is paired with the same arrival time-of-day value in both rows.

These two columns both represent closely related departure timestamps for the same trip segment: one looks like a scheduled departure time and the other an actual/updated departure time. Their meanings are semantically linked because both describe departure for the same underlying flight event.

These two columns both represent closely related arrival times for the same trip segment: one looks like a scheduled arrival time and the other an actual/updated arrival time. Their meanings are semantically linked because both describe arrival for the same underlying flight event.

The booking/travel site name appears associated with a site-specific numeric identifier. In the sample, each source name has its own numeric value, suggesting the numeric field may be an identifier scoped by or dependent on the booking source.
